items.title: Deposed Turkish opposition leader plans new party
items.summary: Özgür Özel, removed as leader of the CHP by court order in May, told parliament he plans to found a new political party — the latest turn in a tense season for Türkiye's opposition.

items.title: US–Iran ceasefire crumbles as fresh strikes rock the Middle East
items.summary: The ceasefire between the US and Iran has broken down, with strikes resuming on both sides. Commercial shipping continues through the Strait of Hormuz, but the region remains on edge.

items.title: 640,000 Syrians have returned home from Turkey since Assad's fall: UNHCR
items.summary: Turkish authorities now put voluntary returns near 700,000 since December 2024, with roughly 2.2 million Syrians still living in Türkiye. UNHCR expects up to a million returns during 2026.

items.title: Having built new lives in Türkiye, many Syrians remain uncertain about returning
items.summary: For many families the decision is no longer about security alone but about jobs, schooling, and the lives they've built over a decade abroad.

items.title: Türkiye takes new steps to encourage returns to Syria
items.summary: From 1 January 2026, Syrians under temporary protection are no longer eligible for free healthcare and pay the same premiums as citizens with social security plans.

items.title: What to expect from immigration services in Türkiye in 2026
items.summary: A practical rundown of this year's residence-permit landscape: the card fee rose to ₺964 in April, financial requirements track 1.5× the net minimum wage, and closed-neighbourhood rules still apply.

items.title: Changes in Turkish immigration law, 2026
items.summary: A legal briefing on this year's amendments affecting foreign residents — worth a read before your next renewal.

items.title: Magnitude-5.0 earthquake strikes Malatya; no major damage reported
items.summary: The quake hit the Battalgazi district early on 18 July. Authorities ran emergency assessments; airports and flight schedules operated normally.

items.title: Türkiye — Level 2: exercise increased caution (U.S. advisory)
items.summary: Level 2 overall, citing terrorism and arbitrary detention risks; Level 4 (do not travel) applies to provinces near the Syrian and Iraqi borders.

items.title: Safety and security — Türkiye travel advice
items.summary: The UK's detailed, regularly updated guidance on demonstrations, terrorism risk, road safety, and earthquake preparedness.

items.title: Rising inflation expectations threaten Turkish lira outlook
items.summary: Market-based inflation expectations for the next 12 months have moved higher, an unfavourable backdrop for the lira even as headline inflation cools.

items.title: Turkey's annual inflation eases to 32.11% in June
items.summary: The lowest reading since March, driven by slower price growth in housing and transport. Policymakers project end-2026 inflation around 26%; the central bank has held its policy rate at 37%.

items.title: Euro reaches ₺53.55 as pressure on the lira continues
items.summary: The euro touched 53.55 lira in early-July trading; the dollar sits near 47.2, down about 17% for the lira over twelve months.
